summary: At exact HEAD af15adafa679545edadf5b053e8842570f7f842b, One-command PR-ready Closure conforms to the intended run-session boundary: the CLI remains an outer composition adapter, the new closure owner has no CLI reverse dependency, production runtime/review owners from PR #377/#382 are reused, guarded autonomy stops before PR creation/merge/waiver/material external effects, and target conformance improves from origin/main 81 violations to 80.
inspection_summary: Inspected the current-head Story, Architecture, Spec, test plan, target model/conformance artifacts, closure/run-session/runtime/review source diff, focused unit/integration/E2E evidence, the real CLI dogfood Run state, and completed predecessor Story evidence. The repaired OCR-S-2/OCR-S-6 disjunction honestly separates a production-shaped available-provider path from the observed pre-mutation missing-capability stop, while OCR-S-8 uses a non-circular two-phase closure and retains explicit human merge authority.
inspection_inputs: git diff origin/main...af15adafa679545edadf5b053e8842570f7f842b -- src/agent-runtime-connectors.js src/independent-review-orchestrator.js src/guarded-run-session.js src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js
source import inspection for run-session owners importing ./cli.js
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the repaired OCR-S-2/OCR-S-6 wording might lower a real-provider requirement into a test-only claim -> Final conclusion: the contract is explicitly disjunctive and evidence-honest; available capability uses the same production action owner in production-shaped E2E, while the real dogfood Run stopped before mutation with provider fallback claude-code, missing workspace_write, unchanged action input/output HEAD, and a persisted runtime_unavailable recovery boundary.

Initial concern: closure changes might reimplement the already merged Production Runtime Connectors or Independent Review Orchestration -> Final conclusion: PR #377 and PR #382 remain completed owner SSOTs; the connector change only propagates the bounded objective, and review changes add closure-required polling, timeout containment, retryable reservation, and current-HEAD checkpoint binding without replacing connector transport or review verdict/lifecycle schemas.

Initial concern: a one-command facade might pull orchestration into cli.js or add a run-session-to-CLI reverse edge -> Final conclusion: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js is the run-session owner, imports no CLI/Gate/connector implementation, accepts bounded callbacks, and current conformance records no new reverse dependency; the pre-existing run-session-to-CLI baseline remains 9 while total violations improve from 81 to 80.

Initial concern: roadmap completion and execute merge could create a circular pre-PR Gate or silently broaden autonomy -> Final conclusion: OCR-S-8 now separates initial candidate evidence from a focused closure commit and fresh current-HEAD reverify/rereview/CI import, and treats execute merge as explicit post-PR human authority/delivery confirmation; the guarded DAG itself rejects PR create, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, and other material external effects.

Initial concern under regression_guard and path_surface_coverage: only the new happy path might be covered -> Final conclusion: current-head evidence covers 254 targeted tests, available-provider success and needs_changes repair, unavailable-provider typed stop/resume/status/cancel, timeout/cancellation containment, human-decision schema, current-HEAD Gate rebinding, CI pending, predecessor reuse, CLI help/JSON surfaces, and exact authority exclusions.
